question: How does Apex Bank protect my data?
answer: Apex Bank uses 256-bit AES encryption for all data at rest and TLS 1.3 for data in transit. We operate a zero-trust security model, meaning every access request is verified regardless of where it originates. Your personal data is stored in UK-based data centres that comply with ISO 27001 standards. We also conduct regular third-party penetration testing and are regulated by the FCA and PRA.

question: What is the process for disputing a transaction?
answer: To dispute a transaction, log into the Apex app or online banking and select the transaction in question. Tap 'Report an issue' and follow the prompts. You can also call our support line on 0800 123 4567. We will investigate within 10 working days and provide a provisional credit to your account if the dispute amount exceeds £50. You will receive updates via the app and email throughout the process.

question: How do I reset my online banking password?
answer: Visit the Apex Bank login page and click 'Forgotten password'. You will need to verify your identity using your registered email address and mobile number. A one-time passcode will be sent to your phone. Once verified, you can set a new password. For security, your new password must be at least 12 characters and include a mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols. If you are locked out after multiple failed attempts, call us on 0800 123 4567.

question: What happens if I miss a loan repayment?
answer: If you miss a loan repayment, we will contact you within 48 hours to discuss your options. A late payment fee of £12 may be applied. If you are experiencing financial difficulty, please contact us as soon as possible — we have a dedicated hardship team that can arrange reduced payments, payment holidays, or restructured repayment plans. Missing payments may affect your credit score, so early communication is always best.

question: How does Apex Bank calculate my credit limit?
answer: Your credit limit is determined by a combination of factors including your annual income, existing credit commitments, credit score from the major reference agencies, employment status, and your history with Apex Bank. We reassess limits periodically and may offer automatic increases if your financial situation improves. You can also request a limit review through the app at any time, though this will involve a soft credit check.

question: What is open banking and does Apex support it?
answer: Open banking allows you to securely share your financial data with authorised third-party providers, such as budgeting apps or other banks, through regulated APIs. Apex Bank fully supports open banking under the UK's PSD2 regulations. You can connect your Apex accounts to any FCA-authorised provider through our app settings. You remain in full control — you can revoke access to any connected service at any time.

question: How do I close my Apex Bank account?
answer: You can close your account by contacting us via the app chat, calling 0800 123 4567, or visiting a branch. Before closing, you will need to clear any outstanding balances and transfer any remaining funds. Direct debits and standing orders will need to be moved to another account. We will ask for the reason for closure to help us improve. Account closure is typically processed within 5 working days.

question: What should I do if my card is lost or stolen?
answer: Freeze your card immediately using the Apex app — tap your card and select 'Freeze'. This blocks all transactions instantly. If you believe the card has been stolen, also report it by calling 0800 123 4567 (available 24/7). We will cancel the card and send a replacement within 3-5 working days, or you can request next-day delivery for £5. Review your recent transactions in the app and report any you do not recognise.

question: How do international transfers work at Apex Bank?
answer: Apex Bank supports international transfers via SWIFT to over 180 countries. You can initiate a transfer through the app or online banking. You will need the recipient's IBAN or account number, their bank's SWIFT/BIC code, and the recipient's full name and address. Transfers to Europe typically arrive within 1-2 working days; other destinations may take 3-5 working days. Exchange rates are set at the time of transfer and a fee of £15 applies per international transfer.

question: What is the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS)?
answer: The FSCS is a UK government-backed scheme that protects your deposits if a bank fails. At Apex Bank, your eligible deposits are protected up to £85,000 per person (or £170,000 for joint accounts). This covers current accounts, savings accounts, and cash ISAs. Investments such as Stocks & Shares ISAs are covered separately up to £85,000. The protection is automatic — you do not need to apply or register.

question: What documents do I need to apply for a mortgage?
answer: To apply for an Apex Bank mortgage you will need: 3 months of payslips (or 2 years of tax returns if self-employed), 3 months of bank statements from all accounts, proof of identity (passport or driving licence), proof of address (utility bill or council tax statement dated within 3 months), proof of deposit (savings statements showing the source of funds), and details of any existing debts or financial commitments. Self-employed applicants should also provide an SA302 tax calculation from HMRC.

question: How do I make a complaint about Apex Bank?
answer: You can make a complaint through the Apex app, by calling 0800 123 4567, by emailing complaints@apexbank.co.uk, or by writing to Apex Bank Complaints, PO Box 1234, London, EC1A 1BB. We aim to resolve complaints within 3 working days. If we need more time, we will acknowledge your complaint within 5 days and provide a final response within 8 weeks. If you are not satisfied with our response, you can escalate to the Financial Ombudsman Service free of charge.

question: Is the Apex Bank app available on all devices?
answer: The Apex Bank app is available for iOS (iPhone and iPad running iOS 15 or later) and Android (version 10 or later). You can download it from the Apple App Store or Google Play Store. The app supports Face ID, Touch ID, and fingerprint authentication for quick and secure access. If your device does not support the app, you can access all features through our mobile-responsive website at online.apexbank.co.uk.

question: Can I apply for Apex Bank products if I am not a UK resident?
answer: Most Apex Bank products require UK residency. You must have a permanent UK address and be resident in the UK for tax purposes. Some products also require a minimum period of UK residency (typically 3 years for mortgages). If you are a British citizen living abroad or a non-UK national with the right to work in the UK, please contact us to discuss your options as eligibility varies by product.

question: How do I recognise and report a scam?
answer: Apex Bank will never ask you to move money to a 'safe account', share your full password or PIN, click links in text messages, or download remote access software. If you receive a suspicious call, text, or email claiming to be from Apex Bank, do not respond. Instead, call us directly on 0800 123 4567 using a different phone if possible. If you have already shared information or transferred money, contact us immediately and we will work with you to secure your account and attempt to recover funds.
